scenario_name: Scenario 18 — Expert Rule Completeness Under Vague Input
justification: All pass criteria met: 10 rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings (exceeds 5 minimum), 5 escalations with clarifying questions plus 7 edge cases surfacing additional unknowns (exceeds 5 minimum), 'demand' explicitly called out as undefined in first escalation, acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written), overall confidence.level is 25 (well under 40 threshold), and no specific numeric values were invented as requirements.

observations.text: Confidence level of 25 is appropriately low for this vague input, with excellent reasoning about why it's low
10 business rules extracted — significantly exceeds the 5 minimum, each with invariant classification and justification
Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from insufficient input
Demand undefined as a concept is the first and most prominent escalation, correctly identified as the critical gap
5 escalations cover demand definition, guardrail thresholds, quote validity, regulatory compliance, and courier compensation — all high-value clarifying questions
7 edge cases surface additional ambiguities (data outages, oscillation, conflicting constraints, fairness, emergencies, zero demand, price-lock)
Invariant classifications are thoughtful — BR-6 (positive price), BR-7 (determinism), and BR-8 (monotonicity) correctly marked as true invariants with strong justifications
Recommendations in escalations are framed as suggestions requiring stakeholder input, not as definitive requirements

Testable assertions in business rules use Given/When/Then-like phrasing, which borders on writing acceptance criteria — though they are clearly labeled as assertions on the rules themselves, not acceptance criteria, and contain undefined placeholders

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context'

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: 8 business rules extracted (exceeds minimum 5); demand explicitly identified as undefined in escalations and low_confidence_areas; acceptance_criteria is an empty array (no Given/When/Then written); confidence.level is 30 (well under 40 threshold); no specific threshold values invented — options in escalations are presented as possibilities, not requirements. At least 5 clarifying questions surfaced across escalations (guardrail thresholds, demand definition, regulatory review, price-lock TTL) and low_confidence_areas (9 items). Minor gap: individual confidence ratings are not inline per rule but are mapped in the confidence.high_confidence_areas/low_confidence_areas sections, which functionally satisfies the intent.

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
confidence.level of 30 with strong reasoning that the input is 'a vision statement, not a spec'
Demand ambiguity surfaced as a dedicated escalation with concrete options for resolution
Guardrail vagueness flagged as the single biggest risk, noting the CEO hedged with 'probably'
Identified 5 assumptions explicitly, all flagged as unstated/implied rather than presented as facts
Edge cases are thoughtful (race conditions, regulatory price-gouging, system outage fallback) and each notes what the spec does NOT clarify
Invariant classifications are well-reasoned — BR-4 (guardrails exist) and BR-5 (monotonicity) and BR-7 (price-lock integrity) marked as invariants with solid justifications

Business rules lack per-rule confidence fields — confidence mapping exists in the confidence section but is not co-located with each rule, making it harder to assess individual rule certainty at a glance

Domain analysis and business_impact sections are fairly detailed for input this vague — borders on producing a 'complete domain analysis' failure mode, though the confidence section and escalations sufficiently caveat the uncertainty

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context'

trials.justification: Meets all pass criteria: 8 business rules extracted with individual confidence/invariant ratings (>=5), 6 escalations serving as clarifying questions (>=5), 'demand' explicitly identified as undefined in multiple places, acceptance_criteria array is empty (no Given/When/Then written), overall confidence.level is 30 (<=40), and no specific values are invented without flagging them as recommendations/options rather than requirements.

observations.text: Empty acceptance_criteria array — correctly refused to write Given/When/Then from vague input
Confidence level of 30 appropriately reflects the massive ambiguity in the input
Demand undefined as a concept is surfaced prominently in escalations, low_confidence_areas, and business rule annotations
8 business rules extracted, each with invariant classification and justification — exceeds the 5-rule minimum
6 escalations with concrete options and recommendations serve as well-structured clarifying questions
Edge cases are thorough and realistic (regulatory price-gouging, rapid oscillation, quote validity)
Where recommendations include specific numbers (e.g., '0.7x to 2.0x'), they are clearly framed as suggestions/options, not as extracted requirements
Caught the 'probably' qualifier on guardrails and escalated it as an ambiguity requiring confirmation

BR-8 testable_assertion says 'NOT DIRECTLY TESTABLE' which is correct but the other rules include testable assertions that reference undefined thresholds — slightly inconsistent about what is testable vs not

Business impact section is quite thorough for a spec Bird deemed too vague for acceptance criteria — borders on producing a 'complete domain analysis' but stays on the right side since it flags unknowns throughout

grader_results.detail: field escalations[*].type equals check failed: expected 'ambiguity', got values: 'missing_context', 'missing_context', 'missing_context', 'missing_context'
